title: coryphantha sneedii var. guadalupensis
intro: Coryphantha sneedii var. guadalupensis, also known as Guadalupe Pincushion Cactus, is a rare and endangered variety of cactus native to the Guadalupe Mountains in Texas, USA. This plant contains medicinal properties, particularly in its latex, which is rich in alkaloids and glycosides that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Guadalupe Pincushion Cactus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory conditions, and act as an antiseptic, among other uses. Traditionally, the latex is harvested and used in herbal preparations, such as teas, salves, and tinctures, although its use requires caution and proper guidance due to potential toxicity.
